policy: A review is valid only for the exact source digest and must replace every substantive generated field.

reviews.evidence: Compared config.yaml arch declaration with the add-on manifest semantics; list is a compatibility whitelist, not a default.

overrides.description: Declares the CPU architectures for which the Supervisor may install InfluxBro. The manifest supports amd64, aarch64, armv7 and armhf; this list is not a runtime setting or default.
overrides.short_description: Limits installation to the four declared CPU architectures.
overrides.prerequisites: Home Assistant Supervisor evaluates the add-on manifest before installation.
overrides.permissions: Changing the manifest requires repository/release access; installation requires Home Assistant administrator access.

inputs.name: arch
inputs.default: N/A - compatibility declaration, not a runtime default
inputs.validation: Every list member must be one of the architectures supported by the Home Assistant add-on schema; the current manifest declares exactly these four values.

overrides.outputs: Supervisor compatibility metadata used to decide whether the add-on can be installed on the host architecture.
overrides.side_effects: No runtime side effect; changing the declaration changes release/install eligibility.

states.success: Supervisor accepts the declaration and evaluates host compatibility.
states.empty: An empty list would make the package unusable and is not the current state.
states.error: Supervisor schema/build validation rejects invalid architecture tokens.
states.retry: Correct the manifest and rebuild/reload the add-on repository.

overrides.security: Architecture eligibility only; it grants no runtime privilege.
dependencies: Home Assistant Supervisor add-on manifest schema.
Container images built for each declared architecture.
overrides.called_by: Home Assistant Supervisor during repository inspection and installation.

reviews.evidence: Reviewed DEFAULT_CFG and api_set_config normalization at app.py:25175-25177.

overrides.description: Selects the active InfluxDB backend mode. The default is v2. Configuration saves lowercase and trim the value; anything except v2 or v3 falls back to v2. Migration switch-over explicitly writes v3.
overrides.short_description: Chooses InfluxDB v2 or v3; invalid values fall back to v2.
overrides.prerequisites: The selected backend must have its corresponding connection settings configured and reachable.
overrides.permissions: Administrative access to InfluxBro configuration; switching to v3 is exposed through the migration workflow.

inputs.name: active_database_mode
inputs.validation: Converted to string, trimmed and lowercased; values outside v2/v3 are replaced with v2 before persistence.

overrides.outputs: Normalized mode string consumed by backend selection and returned in configuration/state responses.
overrides.side_effects: Persists the selected mode and redirects subsequent database operations to the matching configured backend.

states.loading: Shown through the configuration or migration action that changes the mode.
states.success: Normalized v2 or v3 is persisted.
states.error: Unknown input does not raise an error; it safely normalizes to v2.
states.cancel: No separate cancellation after configuration submission.
states.retry: Submit a valid value after correcting backend configuration.

overrides.security: Does not contain credentials, but selecting an unintended target can redirect reads/writes; migration switch-over must remain explicit.
overrides.called_by: Configuration API, migration switch-over, backend selection and API info output.

reviews.evidence: Reviewed the complete route body, _resolve_cfg_path call and browser caller in index.html; adversarial extractor fixtures exclude decorator and unrelated mapping.get calls.

overrides.description: GET /api/reference_source returns the UTF-8 text of an allowed repository/config reference for the source-detail UI. It requires a query path, accepts an optional line number, resolves the path through _resolve_cfg_path and rejects missing or non-file targets.
overrides.short_description: Loads an allowed source file for the reference-detail view.
overrides.prerequisites: Authenticated Ingress/API access and a path accepted by _resolve_cfg_path.
overrides.permissions: Read access of the add-on process, constrained by _resolve_cfg_path.

inputs.name: path
inputs.validation: Trimmed, required, then resolved by _resolve_cfg_path; missing/invalid paths return 400 and absent/non-file paths return 404.

inputs.name: line
inputs.allowed_values: Any integer; response clamps values below 1 to 1.
inputs.validation: Parsed with int(); non-integer input currently raises before a structured 400 response, while output uses max(1, line).

overrides.outputs: JSON {ok, path, line, text} with HTTP 200; structured JSON errors with HTTP 400 or 404 for handled path failures.
overrides.side_effects: Reads one text file into memory; does not write application or InfluxDB data.

states.loading: Reference-detail JavaScript awaits the API call.
states.success: Source text and normalized line are rendered in the detail view.
states.empty: An empty file returns an empty text value with ok=true.
states.error: 400 for missing/rejected path, 404 for missing/non-file target; malformed line is an unhandled conversion error.
states.cancel: No explicit server-side cancellation; browser navigation may abandon the request.
states.retry: The detail view may request the endpoint again with a corrected path/line.

overrides.security: File disclosure boundary: _resolve_cfg_path must confine paths to approved roots. Response can contain operational source/config text and must not be exposed outside authenticated Ingress/API access.

api.summary: Read an allowed source file for the reference-detail UI.
api.description: Requires query parameter path and accepts optional integer line=1. Resolves through _resolve_cfg_path, reads UTF-8 with replacement for invalid bytes, and returns the complete file text.

statusCodes.400: Path missing or rejected by resolver.
statusCodes.404: Resolved target missing or not a file.
statusCodes.500: Malformed line conversion or unhandled file read failure.

timing.bottleneck: Reads and serializes the complete selected file.

errors.cause: Missing/rejected path, absent target, malformed line or file read error.
errors.fix: Use an allowed existing relative path and an integer line.

api.notes: The Flask app has no route-level authentication check; intended protection is Home Assistant Ingress. Direct port exposure must be treated accordingly. Examples contain no private paths or credentials.

reviews.evidence: Compared the complete function-docs job with CI run 29396897262; pytest is installed at an exact version before the fixture-based test module runs, and the job retains read-only repository access.

overrides.description: Runs the function-documentation audit for pull requests and pushes to main. The job checks out the repository, selects Python 3.12, installs exactly pytest 8.4.2 in the ephemeral runner, executes the validator test module, and then requires the complete source-reviewed inventory to validate.
overrides.short_description: Installs pinned pytest and validates the function documentation contract.
overrides.prerequisites: A GitHub-hosted Ubuntu runner with access to GitHub Actions, the repository checkout action, the Python setup action and the Python package index.
overrides.permissions: Read-only repository checkout; no repository write permission or secret is required by this job.

inputs.name: Repository-Stand
inputs.allowed_values: A revision supplied by pull_request or a push to main.
inputs.validation: GitHub Actions resolves the event revision and actions/checkout@v4 checks out that exact workflow run revision.

overrides.outputs: A successful function-docs job only when pytest tests and the strict catalog validator both exit successfully.
overrides.side_effects: Downloads pytest 8.4.2 and its dependencies into the ephemeral runner environment; does not modify runtime, repository contents or persisted application data.

states.loading: The runner checks out source, configures Python and installs the pinned test dependency.
states.success: Both the targeted pytest module and strict inventory validator return exit code zero.
states.empty: N/A - every workflow run has a checked-out repository revision.
states.error: Dependency installation, test or validator failure stops the job and marks the workflow failed.
states.cancel: GitHub Actions can cancel the job; no repository or runtime rollback is needed.
states.retry: Re-run the workflow after correcting the dependency, tests, catalog or review evidence.

overrides.security: The job uses least-privilege read access and no secrets. Pinning pytest to 8.4.2 prevents an unbounded direct test dependency update; package installation remains confined to the ephemeral runner.
overrides.called_by: GitHub Actions on pull_request events and pushes to main.
